format.js:string->empty@const _DURATION_: The 'clock' value of the compact->clock alias emptied to "": the lookup then misses _DURATION and falls through to the same _DURATION.clock default, so 'compact' still renders as clock. Behaviour-identical.
format.js:key->maximumFractionDigits@min: currency's minimumFractionDigits key swapped to a duplicate maximumFractionDigits: Intl's default minimumFractionDigits for a currency already clamps to maximumFractionDigits, so dropping the explicit minimum produces the identical string. Behaviour-identical.
format.js:<-><=@if (Math.abs(decimal - M: bestDenominator's grid-error threshold, < vs <=: the two differ only at an error of exactly 0.01, which no IEEE-754 double reaches — k/2^n is exact but adding the 0.01 double and subtracting it back never recovers the 0.01 double exactly, so the boundary is unreachable. Behaviour-identical over all representable inputs.
format.js:1000000000000.0->1000000000001: trillions divisor 1e12 shifted by +1: the relative perturbation is ~1e-12, below what toFixed(2) resolves for every safe integer — even at MAX_SAFE_INTEGER the second decimal is identical (9007.20 either way). Behaviour-identical over all representable inputs.
trillions divisor 1e12 shifted by -1: as with +1, the ~1e-12 relative perturbation is below toFixed(2) resolution for every safe integer. Behaviour-identical over all representable inputs.
format.js:key->medium@short: { month: \": _DATE_OPTS 'short' key swapped to a duplicate 'medium': the later 'medium' wins so medium is unchanged, and every 'short' reference now misses the table and falls to toLocaleDateString with no options, whose default is exactly the short option set (month/day/year numeric). Behaviour-identical.
the date-style default 'short' emptied to "": an empty style misses _DATE_OPTS and falls through the `?? _DATE_OPTS.short` to short. Behaviour-identical.
the time-style default 'short' emptied to "": an empty style misses _TIME_OPTS and falls through the `?? _TIME_OPTS.short` to short, and !"".includes("24") matches !"short".includes("24") so hour12 is unchanged. Behaviour-identical.
format.js:0->1@[60, 0, null],: the seconds-per-unit of the 'just now' tier shifted 0->1: the tier's unit is null, so it returns 'just now' before the per value is ever divided by. Unused; behaviour-identical.
format.js:0->-1@[60, 0, null],: the seconds-per-unit of the 'just now' tier shifted 0->-1: as above, the null-unit tier returns before using per. Unused; behaviour-identical.
